channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Waymo gets permission to expand robotaxi service to East Bay, North Bay, Sacramento
item.description: Earlier this month, Waymo began offering driverless robotaxi service on Peninsula freeways, and to and from Mineta San Jose International Airport.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: Black Friday scams are everywhere now – here are the 5 best ways to stay safe
item.description: Here are 5 ways to keep fraudsters at bay this Black Friday and ensure your money stays out of scammers’ reach.
item.dc:creator: Alex Blake, TechRadar

item.title: IRS tech chief directs staff to take ‘skills assessment’ ahead of IT reorganization
item.description: The IRS tech chief told staff this summer that the agency needed to “reset and reassess,” because more than 25% of its workforce left the agency this year. The post IRS tech chief directs staff to take ‘skills assessment’ ahead of IT reorganization first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Is AI more appealing than crypto now? A major Bitcoin miner has decided to pivot to AI data centers - here's why
item.description: Bitfarm plans to abandon Bitcoin mining for AI data centers using existing energy capacity despite major losses and volatile markets.
item.dc:creator: Efosa Udinmwen, TechRadar

item.title: Expert Edition: How to assemble the building blocks of impact-driven AI
item.description: AI experts at AWS, Dell, Red Hat and ThunderCat share how the right infrastructure, data governance and workforce readiness can successfully shape mission-driven innovation.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Google is spending $40 billion to build 3 new data centers in Texas
item.description: Google has 44 data centers in operation or in development around the world, but as demand for AI and the need for compute capacity grows, the company is getting started on three more.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: TikTok cares about your well-being and now has a special hub to prove it
item.description: Instead of teens simply putting down their phones to take a break, TikTok wants them to use the app’s new breathing exercises and affirmation journal to improve their well-being.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: CIO Essential Insights: AI Priorities for IT Leaders
item.description: Join host John Gallant for a deep dive into Foundry’s 2025 AI Priorities research, including statistics on AI spending and budgeting, how best to put tools in the hands of employees, the big obstacles companies are facing with AI deployments as well as how best to expedite internal AI projects.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Gluware tackles AI agent coordination with Titan platform
item.description: When Jeff Gray and his co-founders showed their first network automation prototype to a major network equipment manufacturer over a decade ago, the response was dismissive.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta enters power trading to support its AI energy needs
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. is venturing into the complex world of electricity trading, betting it can accelerate the construction of new US power plants that are vital to its artificial intelligence ambitions.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Discount your AI ROI because mileage always varies
item.description: Everyone has been car shopping and noticed the miles per gallon printed on the window sticker. You get excited when you see a number like 35 MPG, but once you drive the car home, your dashboard seems to stay closer to 26.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Downtown San Jose group picks leader at key time for city economy
item.description: The San Jose Downtown Association has picked a new leader as the city seeks a more lively urban core and prepares for a trifecta of mega-events.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: How synthetic data trains AI to solve real problems
item.description: You’ve just finished a strenuous hike to the top of a mountain. You’re exhausted but elated. The view of the city below is gorgeous, and you want to capture the moment on camera. But it’s already quite dark, and you’re not sure you’ll get a good shot.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: Twitch introduces facial age scans in the UK despite privacy concerns
item.description: Twitch now requires UK users to complete a facial age scan to access mature content. This raises privacy concerns and triggers another surge in VPN interest.
item.dc:creator: Monica J. White, TechRadar

item.title: How IT leaders can build successful AI strategies
item.description: The AI gold rush these days is littered with abandoned enterprise projects, with humans — not the technology itself — being blamed for high failure rates of AI projects. Recent data indicates that stagnant AI projects were often the result of poor vision, mismanagement, and a lack of resources.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Server memory prices could double by 2026 as AI demand strains supply
item.description: Server memory prices could double by the end of 2026, driven by manufacturers’ pivot to AI-focused production and Nvidia’s shift to smartphone-style memory that is straining supply further.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: NetOps teams struggle with AI readiness
item.description: As more enterprise organizations adopt artificial intelligence, research reveals a growing gap between AI plans and network preparedness, according to Broadcom.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: SAP touts Microsoft disaster recovery plan for Europe; Analysts doubt it will work
item.description: SAP has cut European deals with Microsoft, Capgemini, and Orange to ostensibly deliver an emergency disaster recovery failover if Microsoft is legally blocked from delivering European services.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: One Tech Tip: Do's and don'ts of using AI to help with schoolwork
item.description: The rise of ChatGPT and other AI systems has transformed education, changing how students learn and study.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan And Jocelyn Gecker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Advocacy groups urge parents to avoid AI toys this holiday season
item.description: Children’s and consumer advocacy groups are urging parents not to buy AI-powered toys during the holiday season.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Ortutay And Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: U.S. Cyber Command has a new Chief Artificial Intelligence Officer
item.description: Novotny steps into the role amid leadership turnover and other turmoil at the military’s top cyber enterprise. The post U.S. Cyber Command has a new Chief Artificial Intelligence Officer first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: Misinformation sites have an open-door policy for AI scrapers
item.description: AI models have a voracious appetite for data. Keeping up to date with information to present to users is a challenge. And so companies at the vanguard of AI appear to have hit on an answer: crawling the web—constantly. But website owners increasingly don’t want to give AI firms free rein.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: How Apple tech can deliver your very own private AI answers
item.description: Yesterday, we looked at how Macs can provide on-premises AI services for you; today we’re going to speculate a little more. Consider this: millions of people use iPhones to access public generative AI (genAI) tools such as ChatGPT, Gemini, and others.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Google releases Gemini 3 with new reasoning and automation features
item.description: Google has launched Gemini 3 and integrated the new AI model into its search engine immediately, aiming to push advanced AI features into consumer and enterprise products faster as competition in the AI market intensifies.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Building a scalable document management system: Lessons from separating metadata and content
item.description: When I was tasked with modernizing our enterprise document management system, I knew the stakes were high. We were managing millions of documents, and our legacy architecture was buckling under the load.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Hands-on with Zed: The IDE built for AI
item.description: Text editors and IDEs tend to inspire devotion from their users. Vim and Emacs have long-standing power-user bases. Visual Studio Code, despite being far newer, has exploded in popularity thanks to its cross-platform support and overall flexibility.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Clojure for Java developers: What you need to know
item.description: Clojure is one of the most fascinating branches of the programming family tree. It unites the high-design heritage of Lisp with practical, modern capabilities. For Java developers, Clojure can be an excellent expansion of their worldview.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Singapore makes the leap to Wi-Fi 7 to boost fan experience
item.description: Recently, Cisco held the Asia-Pacific version of its Cisco Live user event. While this event is typically used to announce new Cisco products, the news payload this year included an interesting customer use case.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: More work for admins as Google patches latest zero-day Chrome vulnerability
item.description: For the third time in recent months, Google has found itself scrambling to fix a potentially serious zero-day flaw in the Chrome browser’s V8 JavaScript engine.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft bets on agentic AI for cloud ops, but analysts doubt the pitch
item.description: Microsoft is betting big on agentic AI to simplify and automate cloud operations by introducing an agentic mode to Azure Copilot that could surface insights and provide recommendations, but not take any actions.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Doomed enterprise AI projects usually lack vision
item.description: Failed implementations of AI technologies are pushing CIOs to step back and try to better understand the technology and its impact before moving ahead, according to analysts and industry experts.
item.dc:creator: Computer World
